counter_proposal.summary: Rather than treating SEPA entry as a standalone achievement, the Ministry of EU Integration would embed it within a comprehensive Chapter 9 closure strategy that simultaneously advances PSD2/PSD3 transposition, open banking regulation, and TIPS Clone implementation, with explicit timelines tied to the 2026 chapter closure target.
key_changes: Establish a dedicated Chapter 9 closure task force co-chaired by the Ministry of EU Integration and the CBCG, with quarterly milestone reporting to the Negotiating Structure.
Link TIPS Clone operational launch (June 2026) explicitly to Chapter 9 benchmarks in the next negotiating position update submitted to Brussels.
Commission a domestic impact assessment of SEPA benefits within 6 months (by August 2026) to quantify savings for SMEs and households, creating an evidence base for the next EC progress report.
Initiate PSD3 regulatory gap analysis immediately, ensuring Montenegro does not fall behind on the evolving EU payments acquis even as it implements PSD2.
Develop a regional SEPA mentorship programme funded through IPA multi-country allocations, positioning Montenegro as the Western Balkans payments integration leader.
expected_benefits: Faster provisional closure of Chapter 9 (Financial Services), potentially achievable by late 2026 or early 2027.
Stronger negotiating position by demonstrating that Montenegro proactively aligns with evolving EU acquis (PSD3), not just current requirements.
Quantified domestic benefits data that can be used to build public support for the accession process and counter EU-skeptic narratives.
Regional leadership role that reinforces Montenegro's frontrunner status and generates goodwill from both the Commission and neighbouring countries.
More effective use of IPA III funds through a coordinated approach rather than piecemeal project-based assistance.
counter_proposal.estimated_feasibility: High feasibility. The institutional infrastructure is already in place through the CBCG and the EU Negotiating Structure. The SEPA success demonstrates strong implementation capacity. The main risk is political bandwidth, as the government is simultaneously managing reforms across multiple chapters. However, the financial services reforms are largely technical and regulatory in nature, making them less politically contentious than Chapters 23 and 24.
counter_proposal.summary_mne: Umjesto da se pristupanje SEPA-i tretira kao izdvojen uspjeh, Ministarstvo za evropske integracije bi ga ugradilo u sveobuhvatnu strategiju zatvaranja Poglavlja 9, koja istovremeno unapređuje transpoziciju PSD2/PSD3, regulativu otvorenog bankarstva i implementaciju TIPS Clone-a, s jasnim rokovima vezanim za cilj zatvaranja poglavlja 2026. godine.
key_changes_mne: Uspostaviti posebnu radnu grupu za zatvaranje Poglavlja 9 kojom zajednički predsjedavaju Ministarstvo za evropske integracije i CBCG, uz kvartalno izvještavanje o ključnim prekretnicama Pregovaračkoj strukturi.
Operativno lansiranje TIPS Clone-a (jun 2026.) eksplicitno povezati s mjerilima Poglavlja 9 u narednom ažuriranju pregovaračke pozicije dostavljene Briselu.

Naručiti procjenu domaćeg uticaja benefita SEPA-e u roku od 6 mjeseci (do avgusta 2026.) radi kvantifikacije uštedina za MSP i domaćinstva, čime se stvara baza dokaza za naredni izvještaj EK o napretku.
Odmah pokrenuti analizu regulatornih nedostataka u pogledu PSD3, kako Crna Gora ne bi zaostajala za razvijajućim platnim akvisom EU dok istovremeno implementira PSD2.
Razviti regionalni program mentorstva u oblasti SEPA-e finansiran kroz IPA višeземљske alokacije, pozicionirajući Crnu Goru kao lidera u platnoj integraciji Zapadnog Balkana.
expected_benefits_mne: Brže privremeno zatvaranje Poglavlja 9 (Finansijske usluge), potencijalno ostvarivo krajem 2026. ili početkom 2027. godine.
Jača pregovaračka pozicija kroz demonstriranje da se Crna Gora proaktivno usklađuje s razvojnim akvisom EU (PSD3), a ne samo s trenutnim zahtjevima.
Kvantifikovani podaci o domaćim benefitima koji se mogu koristiti za izgradnju javne podrške procesu pristupanja i suprotstavljanje EU-skeptičnim narativima.
Uloga regionalnog lidera koja učvršćuje status Crne Gore kao predvodnika i generiše dobru volju kako Komisije, tako i susjednih zemalja.
Efikasnije korišćenje sredstava IPA III kroz koordinisani pristup, umjesto fragmentirane projektne pomoći.
counter_proposal.estimated_feasibility_mne: Visoka izvodljivost. Institucionalna infrastruktura već postoji kroz CBCG i Pregovaračku strukturu za EU. Uspjeh u oblasti SEPA-e svjedoči o snažnom kapacitetu implementacije. Glavni rizik je politički kapacitet, s obzirom na to da Vlada istovremeno upravlja reformama u okviru više poglavlja. Međutim, reforme u oblasti finansijskih usluga su uglavnom tehničke i regulatorne prirode, što ih čini manje politički spornim od Poglavlja 23 i 24.

assessments.summary_mne: Ulazak Crne Gore u SEPA i rezultujuća transformacija troškova prekograničnih plaćanja predstavlja jedno od najopipljivijih i najmjerljivijih dostignuća u procesu evropskih integracija posljednjih godina. Izvještaj Svjetske banke potvrđuje da je Crna Gora prešla put od najlošijeg do najboljeg učesnika u Zapadnom Balkanu po pitanju troškova plaćanja prema EU, direktno doprinijevši privremenom zatvaranju Poglavlja 4 (Slobodno kretanje kapitala) u decembru 2025. godine, napredovanju usklađenosti u okviru Poglavlja 9 (Finansijske usluge) i demonstriranju praktičnih koristi od preuzimanja pravne tekovine EU. Ovo je model primjer kako regulatorno usklađivanje prerasta u konkretne ekonomske koristi za građane i privredu, i jača kredibilitet Crne Gore kao vodećeg kandidata za članstvo u EU.
assessments.executive_summary_mne: Ovo je izrazito pozitivan razvoj događaja za proces evropskih integracija. Ulazak Crne Gore u SEPA direktno je omogućio privremeno zatvaranje Poglavlja 4 i unapređuje usklađenost u okviru Poglavlja 9, istovremeno donoseći mjerljive ekonomske koristi koje potvrđuju vrijednost reformskog procesa pristupanja. Ministarstvo preporučuje nadogradnju na ovaj zamah ubrzanjem implementacije TIPS Clone sistema i korišćenjem uspjeha SEPA-e kao obrasca za reforme u preostalim otvorenim poglavljima.
key_concerns_mne: Pogl. 9 Finansijske usluge -- Iako je integracija u SEPA značajan napredak, Poglavlje 9 ostaje otvoreno. Konačno zatvaranje zahtijeva preuzimanje direktiva PSD2/PSD3, okvira za otvoreno bankarstvo i šire usklađivanje infrastrukture finansijskog tržišta, ne samo platnih sistema.

Pogl. 4 Slobodno kretanje kapitala -- Privremeno zatvaranje je dobrodošlo, ali njegova održivost zavisi od dosljedne primjene standarda za sprečavanje pranja novca i mjera liberalizacije kretanja kapitala koje su bile sastavni dio mjerila za zatvaranje.

Rizik implementacije TIPS Clone sistema -- Sistem plaćanja u realnom vremenu dostupan 24/7 (TIPS Clone), predviđen za juni 2026. godine, od ključnog je značaja za održavanje dinamike napretka. Kašnjenja bi mogla narušiti narativ o napretku i uticati na mjerila za Poglavlje 9.

Koncentracija u bankarskom sektoru -- Malo bankarsko tržište Crne Gore s ograničenom konkurencijom znači da ograničenja naknada mogu zahtijevati stalnu regulatornu primjenu kako bi se spriječio povratak troškova na više nivoe nakon što početna politička pažnja splasne.

Zavisnost od regionalnog prelivanja efekata -- Puni benefiti SEPA-e zavise od toga da i ostale ekonomije Zapadnog Balkana završe integraciju. Troškovi plaćanja unutar regiona ostaju viši od troškova plaćanja usmjerenih ka EU, što ograničava ciljeve Zajedničkog regionalnog tržišta.
recommendations_mne: Ubrzati preuzimanje direktiva PSD2/PSD3 i regulatornog okvira za otvoreno bankarstvo kako bi se nadogradilo na uspjeh SEPA-e i napredovalo prema privremenom zatvaranju Poglavlja 9 (Finansijske usluge) do kraja 2026. godine.

Osigurati da projekat TIPS Clone ostane u planiranim rokovima za pokretanje u junu 2026. godine putem namjenskog praćenja i planiranja za vanredne situacije, uz redovno izvještavanje o napretku Delegaciji EU u Podgorici.

Iskoristiti uspješnu priču o SEPA-i kao obrazac i komunikacijski alat za druga poglavlja koja zahtijevaju intenzivne reforme -- dokazivanje da usklađivanje s pravnom tekovinom EU donosi mjerljive koristi pomaže izgradnji domaće političke podrške za teže reforme u Poglavljima 23 i 24.

Istaknuti nalaze izvještaja Svjetske banke u doprinosu Crne Gore narednom godišnjem izvještaju o napretku Evropske komisije, kao i u bilateralnim razgovorima s predsjedavajućim Savjeta EU, kako bi se ojačao status vodeće kandidatske zemlje.
Ojačati institucionalne kapacitete CBCG za kontinuirani nadzor pružalaca platnih usluga u skladu s pravilima SEPA-e, korišćenjem sredstava tehničke pomoći iz IPA III za period 2025–2027.
Zalagati se u okviru Berlinskog procesa za ubrzanu integraciju svih ekonomija Zapadnog Balkana u SEPA, pozicionirajući Crnu Goru kao regionalni centar znanja i pružaoca tehničke pomoći.

debate.decision_id: World Bank: Montenegro Now Has Lowest Cross-Border Payment Costs to EU After SEPA Entry
debate.consensus_summary: All eight ministries assess SEPA entry as a positive or strongly positive development for Montenegro. There is unanimous agreement that the reduction in cross-border payment costs from the highest to the lowest in the Western Balkans validates the multi-year investment in payment system modernization. Every ministry acknowledges the significance of this milestone for EU accession, with Finance, Economy, Tourism, and EU Integration rating it 9 out of 10. The projected EUR 38 million in annual savings (0.54% of GDP), the improvement in transaction speed from 6% to 63% completing within 24 hours, and the strengthening of Montenegro's frontrunner EU candidate status are universally recognized benefits. All ministries also agree that the TIPS Clone instant payment system scheduled for mid-2026 must remain on track. Furthermore, there is cross-ministry consensus that anti-money laundering enforcement capacity must be urgently strengthened to manage increased cross-border payment volumes, with Interior, Justice, and Finance all flagging MONEYVAL's finding of near-zero money laundering convictions as a critical vulnerability.
disagreements: The degree to which SEPA-driven financial integration poses security risks: the Ministry of Interior and Ministry of Justice emphasize that Montenegro's near-zero money laundering conviction rate, combined with a tenfold increase in fraud risk from instant payments, creates a serious vulnerability that could partially offset economic gains. The Ministry of Finance and Ministry of EU Integration, while acknowledging AML concerns, frame them as manageable and subordinate to the overwhelming economic and accession benefits.

Whether SEPA benefits will reach all segments of society equally: the Ministry of Economy warns that larger firms and coastal businesses will capture savings faster than SMEs in underdeveloped northern regions, and questions whether micro-enterprises see proportional cost reductions. The Ministry of Finance and EU Integration focus on aggregate macroeconomic gains without detailed distributional analysis.

The risk of SEPA integration facilitating brain drain: the Ministry of Education argues that reduced financial friction may marginally accelerate permanent emigration of students, researchers, and health workers by making it easier to support families remotely. The Ministry of Tourism and Ministry of Economy see the same ease of transfer as a competitive advantage for attracting digital nomads, foreign investors, and diaspora engagement.

Environmental implications of accelerated economic activity: the Ministry of Environment warns that faster investment flows enabled by financial integration could accelerate coastal degradation, habitat loss, and construction pressure without adequate environmental safeguards. The Ministry of Tourism acknowledges this risk but frames SEPA as net positive for sustainable tourism development.

Whether banking sector fee revenue losses warrant compensatory measures: the Ministry of Finance projects EUR 50 million in foregone bank fee income by 2028, reducing corporate tax receipts by EUR 4.5-5 million annually, and suggests transitional support. Other ministries view this as an acceptable cost that should not dilute the consumer benefit narrative.

The adequacy of data protection frameworks for SEPA-enabled open banking: the Ministry of Justice raises concerns that PSD2 data-sharing requirements have not been independently verified for GDPR-equivalent compliance, while other ministries do not address this dimension.
debate.debate_transcript:
PARLIAMENTARY DEBATE ON WORLD BANK SEPA REPORT -- SKUPSTINA CRNE GORE

Session opened by the Speaker of Parliament.

--- OPENING: GOVERNMENT PRESENTATION ---

The ruling Europe Now! (Pokret Evropa sad) coalition presents the World Bank report as validation of its signature economic reform. The coalition notes that when Prime Minister Spajic described SEPA entry on 7 October 2025 as Montenegro "truly becoming part of the Single European Market," this was not mere rhetoric -- the World Bank has now confirmed it with hard data. Montenegro moved from the worst to the best performer in the Western Balkans on cross-border payment costs. Transfer costs for EUR 5,000 transactions fell to 0.04-0.05%, and for EUR 20,000 transactions to below 0.01%. The coalition frames this as proof that its governance model -- focused on digitalisation, EU integration, and institutional modernisation -- delivers tangible results for citizens and businesses.

The coalition highlights that the Central Bank's fee cap regulation ensures savings reach consumers directly: the first daily transfer up to EUR 200 is now free, electronic transfers up to EUR 20,000 carry a maximum fee of EUR 1.99, and the banking sector's foregone fee income of EUR 13.9 million translates into direct savings for citizens and the economy. The coalition points to the Ministry of Finance's projection of EUR 38 million in annual savings and the Ministry of EU Integration's assessment that Chapter 4 (Free Movement of Capital) was provisionally closed in December 2025 partly on the strength of SEPA implementation.

URA (Gradjanski pokret URA), as junior coalition partner, expresses strong support but introduces environmental conditionality into the debate. URA notes the Ministry of Environment's assessment that SEPA is only rated 6 out of 10 -- the lowest score among all ministries -- because faster investment flows could accelerate coastal degradation and construction pressure in environmentally sensitive zones. URA calls for a portion of economy-wide savings to be dedicated to the Eco Fund for Chapter 27 compliance, and insists that any broader economic strategy built on SEPA must include environmental conditionality. URA also highlights the Ministry of Tourism's warning about speculative coastal real estate development threatening the Kotor Bay UNESCO site and housing affordability.

--- DPS-LED OPPOSITION RESPONSE ---

The DPS-led opposition (Demokratska partija socijalista and Socijaldemokratska partija) opens by acknowledging that SEPA entry is a positive achievement but immediately challenges the ruling coalition's attempt to claim exclusive credit. The opposition notes that the legislative groundwork for SEPA -- including transposition of PSD1, PSD2, the Payment Accounts Directive, and the Interchange Fee Regulation -- was a multi-year process involving cross-party parliamentary support. The opposition recalls that Montenegro's SEPA application process began well before the current government took office.

The DPS bloc then pivots to what it calls the "security deficit behind the celebration." Citing the Ministry of Interior's assessment, the opposition highlights that Montenegro has achieved only two money laundering convictions according to MONEYVAL, while the European Banking Authority estimates fraud risk in instant payments is up to ten times higher than conventional transfers. The opposition asks how the government can celebrate faster payments when the Financial Intelligence Unit lacks the staff and technology to handle the projected increase in suspicious transaction reports.

SDP reinforces this by citing the Ministry of Justice's finding that average case resolution in Montenegrin courts stands at 739 days, with the Supreme Court backlog at 93%. SDP argues that the government has built a high-speed financial highway but left the traffic police woefully understaffed. SDP calls for the establishment of specialized financial crime courts or chambers and demands a timeline for tripling money laundering prosecutions as recommended by the Ministry of Interior.

The DPS bloc also raises distributional concerns. Referencing the Ministry of Economy's assessment, the opposition notes that benefits are concentrated on EU-facing transactions while intra-Western Balkans payment costs remain disproportionately high. The opposition asks what the government is doing for small businesses in Pljevlja, Rozaje, and Bijelo Polje that trade primarily with Serbia and Kosovo, not with the EU. The opposition further notes that over half of FDI (EUR 455 million of EUR 890 million in 2024) flows to real estate rather than productive sectors, and SEPA alone will not redirect this.

On brain drain, the DPS bloc cites the Ministry of Education's concern that reduced financial friction may accelerate permanent emigration. With a third of Montenegrin citizens already living abroad and 50% of young people expressing a desire to leave, the opposition argues that making it easier to send money home may reduce the pressure to return. The opposition demands a comprehensive Brain Circulation Strategy paired with funded return fellowships, as recommended by the Ministry of Education.

--- DF/DNP BLOC RESPONSE ---

The Demokratski front and DNP bloc frames the SEPA discussion through its characteristic sovereignty lens. The bloc acknowledges the practical benefits of lower transaction costs but raises fundamental concerns about deepening euroisation without monetary sovereignty. Citing the Ministry of Finance's own assessment that Montenegro uses the euro without ECB membership, the DF/DNP bloc argues that SEPA integration further locks Montenegro into a monetary framework where EU payment regulation changes will apply automatically without Montenegrin input or consent.

The DF/DNP bloc expresses particular concern about the security dimension, though from a different angle than the DPS opposition. The bloc references the Ministry of Interior's finding that transnational organised crime networks from Turkey, Georgia, Russia, and Eastern Europe are already embedded in Montenegro's financial system, particularly through real estate. The bloc argues that faster and cheaper payment channels will primarily benefit these networks unless law enforcement capacity is dramatically expanded.

The DF/DNP bloc also questions the narrative of EU integration as inherently beneficial, noting that Montenegro is being integrated into European payment infrastructure while still lacking basic sovereignty tools -- no central bank lender-of-last-resort capacity, no monetary policy independence, and no seat at the ECB table. The bloc argues that true economic sovereignty requires a seat at the decision-making table, not merely compliance with rules set by others.

On the topic of the banking sector, the DF/DNP bloc notes that the EUR 50 million in foregone bank fee income represents a transfer from Montenegrin banks -- many of which are domestically owned -- to consumers of cross-border services who are disproportionately foreign investors and coastal businesses. The bloc questions whether this transfer serves the national interest.

--- MINORITY PARTY INTERVENTIONS ---

Bosnjacka stranka and the Albanian minority parties (FORCA, Albanian Alternative) welcome SEPA integration as a step toward European standards but raise specific concerns about equitable access. These parties highlight the Ministry of Economy's warning that northern municipalities and rural areas lack the digital banking infrastructure to fully exploit SEPA advantages. They note that their constituencies, concentrated in Rozaje, Plav, Ulcinj, and other municipalities with significant minority populations, risk being left behind if the government does not invest in digital banking access and financial literacy programs.

The minority parties also welcome the potential for cheaper remittance flows, noting that diaspora communities from these municipalities send significant funds home. However, they caution that the benefits are limited to EU-directed transfers and call on the government to negotiate similar cost reductions for transfers from Turkey, the Gulf states, and other destinations where Montenegrin minority diaspora communities reside.

The minority parties express support for the Ministry of Education's recommendation to establish a SEPA-enabled Diaspora Academic Engagement Program and call for this to specifically target minority-language academic programs and researchers.

--- COMMITTEE DELIBERATIONS ---

The Committee on Economy, Finance, and Budget notes broad support for the SEPA achievement across all parliamentary blocs but identifies three areas requiring government action:

First, the security gap. Five of eight ministries (Interior, Justice, Finance, Economy, and Health) raise concerns about anti-money laundering capacity. The Committee recommends that the government present a comprehensive financial crime enforcement plan within 90 days, including a timeline for establishing a dedicated Financial Crimes Task Force, deploying AI-powered real-time transaction monitoring in all banks, and tripling money laundering prosecutions within two years.

Second, distributional equity. The Committee notes that four ministries (Economy, Education, Tourism, and the minority parties) raise concerns about uneven benefit distribution between coastal and northern regions, between large firms and SMEs, and between EU-facing and regionally-facing businesses. The Committee recommends a targeted SME digitalisation program for northern municipalities and a study on SEPA impact on micro-enterprises.

Third, the TIPS Clone implementation timeline. The Committee notes that three ministries (Finance, Economy, and EU Integration) identify the mid-2026 TIPS Clone launch as critical. The Committee recommends quarterly progress reporting to Parliament and contingency planning for delays.

The Committee on European Integration expresses strong satisfaction with the SEPA achievement and notes the Ministry of EU Integration's assessment that this strengthens Montenegro's frontrunner EU candidate status. The Committee recommends that the World Bank findings be prominently featured in Montenegro's contribution to the next European Commission progress report. The Committee also recommends accelerating PSD2/PSD3 transposition to advance Chapter 9 toward provisional closure by end of 2026.

The Committee on Security and Defence echoes the Ministry of Interior's concerns and recommends an independent security impact assessment of SEPA integration within six months. The Committee notes MONEYVAL's February 2024 finding that real estate investment has become the most frequent method for money laundering in Montenegro and warns that faster, cheaper payment channels could exacerbate this vulnerability without urgent countermeasures.

--- CROSS-BLOC CONSENSUS ---

Despite the adversarial dynamics, several points command cross-bloc agreement. All blocs acknowledge that SEPA entry is a genuine achievement that benefits Montenegro's economy and EU accession prospects. All blocs agree that anti-money laundering enforcement capacity must be urgently strengthened. All blocs support the TIPS Clone implementation timeline. And all blocs recognize that the benefits must be distributed more equitably across regions and business sizes.

The primary disagreements are not about the merits of SEPA itself but about: (a) whether the ruling coalition deserves exclusive credit, (b) the adequacy of security preparations, (c) the sovereignty implications of deeper euroisation, and (d) whether the government has a plan to ensure northern and rural communities share in the benefits.

--- VOTING INDICATION ---

Based on the debate, the parliamentary majority (Europe Now!/URA coalition) would vote to endorse the government's SEPA implementation as strongly positive. The DPS-led opposition would vote to acknowledge the achievement as positive while attaching conditions on security enforcement and distributional equity. The DF/DNP bloc would abstain or vote to note the achievement with reservations on sovereignty and security grounds. Minority parties would vote positive with conditions on equitable regional access.

The Parliament's collective assessment is strongly positive, reflecting the unanimous ministerial view that SEPA entry is beneficial, tempered by cross-party insistence on addressing the security, distributional, and sovereignty concerns identified in the debate.
debate.consensus_summary_mne: Svih osam ministarstva ocjenjuje ulazak u SEPA kao pozitivan ili izrazito pozitivan razvoj za Crnu Goru. Postoji jednoglasna saglasnost da smanjenje troškova prekograničnih plaćanja – s najviših na najniže u Zapadnom Balkanu – potvrđuje višegodišnju investiciju u modernizaciju platnog sistema. Svako ministarstvo prepoznaje značaj ovog prekretnice za pristupanje EU, pri čemu su Ministarstvo finansija, Ministarstvo ekonomije, Ministarstvo turizma i Ministarstvo za evropske integracije ocijenile proces ocjenom 9 od 10. Projicirane godišnje uštedine od 38 miliona EUR (0,54% BDP-a), poboljšanje brzine transakcija – s 6% na 63% završenih u roku od 24 sata – te jačanje statusa Crne Gore kao predvodnika u procesu kandidature za EU, opšteprepoznate su kao koristi. Sva ministarstva se slažu i da sistem trenutnih plaćanja TIPS Clone, planiran za sredinu 2026. godine, mora ostati na zadanom rasporedu. Pored toga, postoji međuresorni konsenzus da kapacitete za borbu protiv pranja novca treba hitno ojačati kako bi se upravljalo povećanim obimom prekograničnih plaćanja – Ministarstvo unutrašnjih poslova, Ministarstvo pravde i Ministarstvo finansija zajednički su istakli nalaz MONEYVAL-a o gotovo nultom broju osuđujućih presuda za pranje novca kao kritičnu ranjivost.
disagreements_mne: Stepen u kome finansijska integracija pokrenuta SEPA-om predstavlja bezbjednosne rizike: Ministarstvo unutrašnjih poslova i Ministarstvo pravde naglašavaju da gotovo nulta stopa osuđujućih presuda za pranje novca u Crnoj Gori, u kombinaciji s deseterostrukim povećanjem rizika od prevare u slučaju trenutnih plaćanja, stvara ozbiljnu ranjivost koja bi mogla djelimično poništiti ekonomske dobitke. Ministarstvo finansija i Ministarstvo za evropske integracije, mada prepoznaju zabrinutosti u pogledu borbe protiv pranja novca, tretiraju ih kao upravive i podređene dominantnim ekonomskim prednostima i koristima za pristupanje EU.

Da li će koristi od SEPA-e jednako dosegnuti sve segmente društva: Ministarstvo ekonomije upozorava da će veće firme i priobalna preduzeća brže ostvariti uštedine od malih i srednjih preduzeća u nerazvijenim sjevernim regijama, te dovodi u pitanje da li mikropreduzeća bilježe proporcionalna smanjenja troškova. Ministarstvo finansija i Ministarstvo za evropske integracije fokusiraju se na agregatne makroekonomske dobitke bez detaljne analize distribucijskih efekata.

Rizik da SEPA integracija podstiče odliv mozgova: Ministarstvo prosvjete tvrdi da smanjeno finansijsko trenje može marginalno ubrzati trajnu emigraciju studenata, istraživača i zdravstvenih radnika, olakšavajući im daljinsko finansijsko pomaganje porodicama. Ministarstvo turizma i Ministarstvo ekonomije posmatraju istu lakoću transfera kao konkurentsku prednost za privlačenje digitalnih nomada, stranih investitora i angažovanje dijaspore.

Ekološke implikacije ubrzane ekonomske aktivnosti: Ministarstvo ekologije upozorava da bi brži investicioni tokovi omogućeni finansijskom integracijom mogli ubrzati degradaciju priobalja, gubitak staništa i pritisak na izgradnju bez adekvatnih ekoloških zaštitnih mjera. Ministarstvo turizma prepoznaje ovaj rizik, ali SEPA-u okvirno posmatra kao neto pozitivnu za razvoj održivog turizma.

Da li gubici prihoda bankarskog sektora od naknada opravdavaju kompenzatorne mjere: Ministarstvo finansija projektuje 50 miliona EUR izgubljenih bankarskih prihoda od naknada do 2028. godine, što smanjuje prihode od poreza na dobit za 4,5–5 miliona EUR godišnje, te predlaže prijelaznu podršku. Ostala ministarstva to smatraju prihvatljivim troškom koji ne bi trebalo da umanjuje narativ o koristima za potrošače.

Adekvatnost okvira zaštite podataka za otvoreno bankarstvo omogućeno SEPA-om: Ministarstvo pravde izražava zabrinutost da zahtjevi za razmjenu podataka u skladu s PSD2 nisu nezavisno provjereni radi usaglašenosti s GDPR standardima, dok ostala ministarstva ne razmatraju ovaj aspekt.
debate.debate_transcript_mne:
PARLAMENTARNA RASPRAVA O IZVJEŠTAJU SVJETSKE BANKE O SEPA-I -- SKUPŠTINA CRNE GORE

Sjednicu je otvorio predsjednik Skupštine.

--- UVOD: PREZENTACIJA VLADE ---

Koalicija na vlasti Evropa sad! (Pokret Evropa sad) predstavlja izvještaj Svjetske banke kao potvrdu svog ključnog ekonomskog reformskog projekta. Koalicija ističe da kada je predsjednik Vlade Spajić 7. oktobra 2025. godine opisao ulazak u SEPA kao trenutak kada je Crna Gora "zaista postala dio jedinstvenog evropskog tržišta", nije bila riječ o pukoj retorici – Svjetska banka to je sada potvrdila konkretnim podacima. Crna Gora prešla je put od najlošijeg do najboljeg izvođača u Zapadnom Balkanu po troškovima prekograničnih plaćanja. Troškovi transfera za transakcije od 5.000 EUR pali su na 0,04–0,05%, a za transakcije od 20.000 EUR – ispod 0,01%. Koalicija ovo predstavlja kao dokaz da njen model upravljanja – zasnovan na digitalizaciji, evropskim integracijama i institucionalnoj modernizaciji – donosi opipljive rezultate građanima i privrednim subjektima.

Koalicija naglašava da uredba Centralne banke o ograničenju naknada osigurava da uštedine direktno dostignu potrošače: prvi dnevni transfer do 200 EUR sada je besplatan, elektronski transferi do 20.000 EUR nose maksimalnu naknadu od 1,99 EUR, a izgubljena prihodi bankarskog sektora od naknada u iznosu od 13,9 miliona EUR direktno se pretvaraju u uštedine za građane i privredu. Koalicija ukazuje na projekciju Ministarstva finansija o godišnjim uštedinama od 38 miliona EUR, te na procjenu Ministarstva za evropske integracije da je Poglavlje 4 (Slobodno kretanje kapitala) privremeno zatvoreno u decembru 2025. godine, dijelom zahvaljujući snazi implementacije SEPA-e.

URA (Građanski pokret URA), kao mlađi koalicioni partner, izražava snažnu podršku, ali u raspravu uvodi ekološku uslovljenost. URA napominje da Ministarstvo ekologije SEPA-u ocjenjuje sa svega 6 od 10 – što je najniža ocjena među svim ministarstvima – budući da bi brži investicioni tokovi mogli ubrzati degradaciju priobalja i pritisak na izgradnju u ekološki osjetljivim zonama. URA poziva da se dio uštedina na nivou čitave ekonomije namijeni Eko fondu radi usklađenosti s Poglavljem 27, te insistira da svaka šira ekonomska strategija zasnovana na SEPA-i mora sadržavati ekološku uslovljenost. URA takođe skreće pažnju na upozorenje Ministarstva turizma o špekulativnom razvoju nekretnina na priobaljua koji ugrožava UNESCO lokalitet Bokokotorskog zaliva i dostupnost stanovanja.

--- ODGOVOR OPOZICIJE NA ČELU S DPS-OM ---

Opozicija predvođena DPS-om (Demokratska partija socijalista i Socijaldemokratska partija) otvara raspravu priznavanjem da je ulazak u SEPA pozitivan rezultat, ali odmah osporava pokušaj vladajuće koalicije da prisvoji isključivu zaslugu. Opozicija ističe da je zakonodavni temelj za SEPA – uključujući transpoziciju PSD1, PSD2, Direktive o platnim računima i Uredbe o međubankarskim naknadama – bio višegodišnji proces uz međupartijsku parlamentarnu podršku. Opozicija podsjeća da je proces Crne Gore za prijem u SEPA počeo znatno prije nego što je sadašnja vlada preuzela dužnost.

DPS blok zatim prelazi na ono što naziva "bezbjednosnim deficitom iza proslave". Pozivajući se na procjenu Ministarstva unutrašnjih poslova, opozicija ističe da je Crna Gora postigla samo dvije osuđujuće presude za pranje novca prema nalazima MONEYVAL-a, dok Evropski bankarski organ procjenjuje da je rizik od prevare kod trenutnih plaćanja i do deset puta veći nego kod konvencionalnih transfera. Opozicija pita kako vlada može slaviti brža plaćanja kada Jedinica za finansijsku obavještajnu djelatnost nema dovoljno kadra ni tehnologije za predviđeno povećanje broja prijava sumnjivih transakcija.

SDP podupire ove argumente navodeći nalaz Ministarstva pravde da prosječno rješavanje predmeta u crnogorskim sudovima traje 739 dana, uz 93% zaostalih predmeta u Vrhovnom sudu. SDP tvrdi da je vlada izgradila brzi finansijski koridor, ali ostavila saobraćajnu policiju u ozbiljnom kadrovskom deficitu. SDP poziva na osnivanje specijalizovanih sudova ili vijeća za finansijski kriminal i zahtijeva vremenski plan za utrostručenje krivičnih gonjenja za pranje novca, u skladu s preporukom Ministarstva unutrašnjih poslova.

DPS blok pokreće i distribucijska pitanja. Pozivajući se na procjenu Ministarstva ekonomije, opozicija napominje da su koristi koncentrisane na transakcije usmjerene prema EU, dok troškovi plaćanja unutar Zapadnog Balkana ostaju neproporcionalno visoki. Opozicija pita šta vlada čini za mala preduzeća u Pljevljima, Rožajama i Bijelom Polju koja trguju pretežno sa Srbijom i Kosovom, a ne s EU. Opozicija dalje napominje da više od polovine SDI (455 miliona EUR od 890 miliona EUR u 2024. godini) odlazi u nekretnine, a ne u produktivne sektore, te da SEPA sama po sebi neće promijeniti ovaj trend.

U pogledu odliva mozgova, DPS blok citira zabrinutost Ministarstva prosvjete da smanjeno finansijsko trenje može ubrzati trajnu emigraciju. S obzirom na to da trećina crnogorskih građana već živi u inostranstvu, a 50% mladih izražava želju za odlaskom, opozicija tvrdi da olakšavanje doznaka može smanjiti pritisak za povratak u zemlju. Opozicija zahtijeva sveobuhvatnu strategiju cirkulacije mozgova, praćenu finansiranim stipendijama za povratak, u skladu s preporukom Ministarstva prosvjete.

--- ODGOVOR DF/DNP BLOKA ---

Blok Demokratskog fronta i DNP-a temu SEPA-e sagledava kroz svoju karakterističnu prizmu suvereniteta. Blok prepoznaje praktične prednosti nižih transakcionih troškova, ali izražava suštinske zabrinutosti u pogledu produbljivanja eurizacije bez monetarnog suvereniteta. Pozivajući se na sopstvenu procjenu Ministarstva finansija da Crna Gora koristi euro bez članstva u ESB-u, DF/DNP blok tvrdi da SEPA integracija dodatno učvršćuje Crnu Goru u monetarni okvir u kome će se promjene u EU platnoj regulativi primjenjivati automatski, bez crnogorskog učešća ili saglasnosti.

DF/DNP blok posebnu zabrinutost izražava u pogledu bezbjednosne dimenzije, mada s drugačijeg aspekta u odnosu na DPS opoziciju. Blok se poziva na nalaz Ministarstva unutrašnjih poslova da su transnacionalne mreže organizovanog kriminala iz Turske, Gruzije, Rusije i Istočne Evrope već duboko ukorijenjene u crnogorski finansijski sistem, posebno kroz sektor nekretnina. Blok tvrdi da će brži i jeftiniji kanali plaćanja primarno koristiti ovim mrežama, osim ako se kapaciteti organa za provođenje zakona drastično ne ojačaju.

DF/DNP blok dovodi u pitanje i narativ o EU integraciji kao inherentno korisnoj, napominjući da se Crna Gora integriše u evropsku platnu infrastrukturu, a da i dalje nema osnovne alate suverenosti – nema kapacitet centralnobankarskog zajmodavca u krajnjoj instanci, nema monetarnu nezavisnost i nema mjesto za stolom ESB-a. Blok tvrdi da istinski ekonomski suverenitet podrazumijeva prisustvo na mjestima donošenja odluka, a ne samo ispunjavanje pravila koje su postavili drugi.

Govoreći o bankarskom sektoru, DF/DNP blok napominje da 50 miliona EUR izgubljenih prihoda banaka od naknada predstavlja transfer od crnogorskih banaka – od kojih su mnoge u domaćem vlasništvu – prema korisnicima prekograničnih usluga, koji su nesrazmjerno strani investitori i priobalna preduzeća. Blok dovodi u pitanje da li ovaj transfer služi nacionalnom interesu.

--- NASTUPANJE MANJINSKIH STRANAKA ---

Bošnjačka stranka i albanske manjinske stranke (FORCA, Albanska alternativa) pozdravljaju SEPA integraciju kao korak ka evropskim standardima, ali ukazuju na specifične zabrinutosti u pogledu ravnopravnog pristupa. Ove stranke ističu upozorenje Ministarstva ekonomije da sjevernim opštinama i ruralnim područjima nedostaje digitalna bankarska infrastruktura za potpuno iskorišćavanje prednosti SEPA-e. Napominju da njihova biračka tijela, koncentrisana u Rožajama, Plavu, Ulcinju i drugim opštinama sa značajnim manjinskim stanovništvom, riskiraju da budu izostavljena ako vlada ne uloži u digitalnu dostupnost bankarskih usluga i programe finansijske pismenosti.

Manjinske stranke takođe pozdravljaju potencijal za jeftinije doznake, ističući da dijasporske zajednice iz ovih opština šalju značajna sredstva u zavičaj. Međutim, upozoravaju da su koristi ograničene na transfere usmjerene prema EU i pozivaju vladu da pregovara o sličnim smanjenjima troškova za transfere iz Turske, zemalja Zalivskog savjeta i ostalih destinacija u kojima boravi crnogorska manjinska dijaspora.

Manjinske stranke podržavaju preporuku Ministarstva prosvjete o uspostavljanju Programa akademskog angažmana dijaspore uz podršku SEPA-e i pozivaju da ovaj program posebno targetira akademske programe i istraživače na manjinskim jezicima.

--- RAZMATRANJA U ODBORIMA ---

Odbor za ekonomiju, finansije i budžet konstatuje široku podršku SEPA postignuću u svim parlamentarnim blokovima, ali identifikuje tri oblasti koje zahtijevaju djelovanje vlade:

Prvo, bezbjednosni jaz. Pet od osam ministarstava (unutrašnjih poslova, pravde, finansija, ekonomije i zdravlja) izražava zabrinutost u pogledu kapaciteta za borbu protiv pranja novca. Odbor preporučuje da vlada u roku od 90 dana predstavi sveobuhvatan plan za suzbijanje finansijskog kriminala, koji uključuje vremenski plan za osnivanje namjenske Radne grupe za finansijski kriminal, uvođenje sistema praćenja transakcija u realnom vremenu zasnovanog na vještačkoj inteligenciji u svim bankama i utrostručenje krivičnih gonjenja za pranje novca u roku od dvije godine.

Drugo, distribucijska ravnopravnost. Odbor napominje da četiri ministarstva (ekonomije, prosvjete, turizma i manjinske stranke) ukazuju na zabrinutost zbog neravnomjerne distribucije koristi između priobalnih i sjevernih regija, između velikih firmi i malih i srednjih preduzeća, te između privrednih subjekata orijentisanih prema EU i onih orijentisanih regionalno. Odbor preporučuje ciljani program digitalizacije malih i srednjih preduzeća za sjeverne opštine i studiju o uticaju SEPA-e na mikropreduzeća.

Treće, vremenski plan implementacije TIPS Clone sistema. Odbor napominje da tri ministarstva (finansija, ekonomije i za evropske integracije) identifikuju lansiranje TIPS Clone sistema sredinom 2026. godine kao ključno. Odbor preporučuje kvartalno izvještavanje Skupštini o napretku i planiranje mjera za slučaj kašnjenja.

Odbor za evropske integracije izražava snažno zadovoljstvo SEPA postignućem i konstatuje da procjena Ministarstva za evropske integracije potvrđuje da ovo jača status Crne Gore kao predvodnika u procesu kandidature za EU. Odbor preporučuje da nalazi Svjetske banke budu istaknuto uvršteni u doprinos Crne Gore narednom izvještaju Evropske komisije o napretku. Odbor takođe preporučuje ubrzanje transpozicije PSD2/PSD3 radi napretka Poglavlja 9 ka privremenom zatvaranju do kraja 2026. godine.

Odbor za bezbjednost i odbranu ponavlja zabrinutosti Ministarstva unutrašnjih poslova i preporučuje nezavisnu procjenu bezbjednosnog uticaja SEPA integracije u roku od šest mjeseci. Odbor konstatuje nalaz MONEYVAL-a iz februara 2024. godine da je investiranje u nekretnine postalo najučestaliji metod pranja novca u Crnoj Gori i upozorava da bi brži i jeftiniji platni kanali mogli pogoršati ovu ranjivost bez hitnih protivmjera.

--- MEĐUBLOKOVNI KONSENZUS ---

Uprkos suprotstavljenoj dinamici, nekoliko tačaka uživa međublokovnu saglasnost. Svi blokovi prepoznaju da je ulazak u SEPA istinsko postignuće koje koristi crnogorskoj ekonomiji i perspektivama pristupanja EU. Svi blokovi se slažu da kapacitete za borbu protiv pranja novca treba hitno ojačati. Svi blokovi podržavaju vremenski plan implementacije TIPS Clone sistema. I svi blokovi prepoznaju da koristi moraju biti ravnopravnije distribuirane po regijama i veličinama preduzeća.

Glavna neslaganja ne tiču se merita same SEPA-e, već: (a) da li vladajuća koalicija zaslužuje isključivu zaslugu, (b) adekvatnosti bezbjednosnih priprema, (c) implikacija po suverenitet u kontekstu dublje eurizacije i (d) toga da li vlada ima plan da osigura da sjeverne i ruralne zajednice dijele koristi.

--- NAZNAKA GLASANJA ---

Na osnovu rasprave, parlamentarna većina (koalicija Evropa sad!/URA) glasala bi za potvrdu vladine implementacije SEPA-e kao izrazito pozitivne. Opozicija predvođena DPS-om glasala bi za priznanje postignuća kao pozitivnog, uz prilaganje uslova u pogledu bezbjednosnih mjera i distribucijske ravnopravnosti. DF/DNP blok suzdržao bi se od glasanja ili glasao za konstatovanje postignuća uz rezerve u pogledu suvereniteta i bezbjednosti. Manjinske stranke glasale bi pozitivno uz uslove ravnopravnog regionalnog pristupa.

Kolektivna ocjena Skupštine je izrazito pozitivna, odražavajući jednoglasno ministarsko mišljenje da je ulazak u SEPA koristan, uz međupartijsko insistiranje na rješavanju bezbjednosnih, distribucijskih i suverenitetnih pitanja identifikovanih u raspravi.

blind_spots: This is not a government decision but a retrospective World Bank validation of a policy implemented in October 2025. The ministries treat it as if they are assessing a new decision, when they are really celebrating a report card. No ministry questions why this is on the government agenda as a decision item rather than an informational briefing.

No ministry acknowledges that Montenegro's cost advantage over other Western Balkan SEPA entrants (Albania, North Macedonia, Moldova, which joined simultaneously on October 7, 2025) is largely structural: Montenegro already uses the euro, eliminating currency conversion costs. The 'lowest cost' achievement is partly a pre-existing advantage from euroization, not purely a SEPA implementation triumph.

The EUR 38 million annual savings figure and the EUR 160 million projection (including TIPS Clone) are repeatedly cited as World Bank findings, but appear to originate from the Central Bank of Montenegro's own projections. No ministry critically examines the methodology behind these figures or distinguishes between CBCG estimates and independently verified World Bank data.

No ministry addresses the concentration risk in Montenegro's banking sector -- eleven banks in a market of 620,000 people with limited competition. Fee caps may suppress costs temporarily, but without genuine competition, banks may compensate through higher charges on non-SEPA domestic services, account maintenance fees, or lending margins. The sustainability of regulatory price-setting without market competition is unexamined.

None of the ministries discuss the implications of Montenegro's 17% public debt exposure to China's Eximbank loan alongside deeper EU financial integration. Whether cheaper EU capital flows could strategically help Montenegro refinance away from non-EU creditors, reducing geopolitical dependency, is a missed strategic point.

No ministry examines what concrete AML enforcement improvements have actually been implemented since the damning February 2024 MONEYVAL report. The AML law amendments adopted in February 2025 are mentioned by Justice, but there is no assessment of whether prosecutorial behavior has changed, whether the Financial Intelligence Unit has been resourced, or whether the two-conviction baseline has improved in the 18 months since the MONEYVAL evaluation.

The World Bank report covers B2B payments specifically, but several ministries (Education, Health, Tourism) extrapolate benefits to consumer-level and remittance transactions without distinguishing between the B2B findings and SEPA's broader consumer impact. This conflation inflates the perceived scope of the World Bank's validation.

No ministry addresses the digital divide and financial literacy gap. SEPA benefits flow through digital banking channels, yet a significant portion of Montenegro's population, particularly elderly citizens and those in northern rural areas, may lack the digital literacy or banking access to benefit, potentially widening economic inequality.
critic_report.overall_analysis:
Montenegro's SEPA entry is a genuine achievement that deserves recognition. Moving from the most expensive to the cheapest Western Balkan corridor for EU payments in under a year is a concrete, measurable result that directly benefits businesses and citizens. The World Bank's independent verification lends credibility to the achievement. Chapter 4 (Free Movement of Capital) was provisionally closed in December 2025 partly on the strength of this reform, and the European Payments Council has recognized it as a milestone. This is one of the clearest cases where EU-oriented reform has delivered tangible, quantifiable benefits to the Montenegrin economy.

However, several critical issues temper the celebration.

First, the achievement is partly structural, not purely earned. Montenegro already uses the euro unilaterally, which gives it an inherent cost advantage over Albania (which uses the lek) and North Macedonia (which uses the denar) in SEPA transactions. Currency conversion costs are zero for Montenegro by default. Comparing Montenegro's SEPA costs to countries that must also convert currencies is comparing apples to oranges. The ministries universally fail to acknowledge this, treating the 'lowest cost' finding as purely a triumph of policy implementation.

Second, the elephant in the room is enforcement. MONEYVAL's February 2024 evaluation was devastating: moderate effectiveness in 9 of 11 areas, near-zero money laundering convictions (reportedly only two), and prosecutors preferring asset confiscation over actually prosecuting money laundering. Montenegro's real estate sector, which absorbs more than half of all FDI (EUR 455 million of EUR 890 million in 2024), has been flagged by MONEYVAL and Balkan Insight as the primary vehicle for laundering proceeds from cocaine trafficking by groups like the Kavac and Skaljari clans. Making cross-border payments faster and cheaper without proportionally upgrading AML enforcement creates a wider opening for illicit financial flows. The Ministry of Interior and Ministry of Justice both raised this concern, which is commendable, but neither provides evidence that enforcement has actually improved since the MONEYVAL report. AML law amendments were adopted in February 2025, but legal frameworks were never Montenegro's weakness -- enforcement was.

Third, the projected economic benefits need scrutiny. The EUR 38 million annual savings figure (0.54% of GDP) appears to originate from the Central Bank of Montenegro, not the World Bank report itself. The even more ambitious EUR 160 million projection (2.3% of GDP, including TIPS Clone benefits and per capita income increases of EUR 2,500 over a decade) strains credulity without rigorous methodological backing. For context, Montenegro's GDP is approximately EUR 7.5 billion and its general government deficit is projected to widen to 3.6% of GDP in 2025 according to the IMF. The SEPA savings, while real, are marginal relative to the country's fiscal challenges, including debt service on the EUR 850 million Eurobond issued in March 2025 and the still-outstanding China Eximbank loan comprising 17% of public debt.

Fourth, the TIPS Clone instant payment system, scheduled for June-July 2026 with Bank of Italy cooperation, is critical but carries implementation risk. If it is delayed, the most ambitious economic benefit projections collapse. The EBA has assessed fraud risk in instant credit transfers at up to 10 times higher than conventional transfers. Montenegro's banking sector, with its 11 banks and limited competition in a market of 620,000 people, faces the challenge of performing complete AML screening within the 10-second window required for instant payments. Legacy compliance systems were not built for this.

Fifth, the ministry assessments, while generally thorough, display a celebratory tone that borders on cheerleading. The Ministry of Finance and Ministry of Economy both assign 9/10 scores and project strongly positive outcomes, but neither seriously interrogates whether the fee caps imposed by the CBCG are sustainable in a non-competitive market, or whether banks will simply shift costs to other product lines. The Ministry of EU Integration's 9/10 score is arguably justified given the direct connection to Chapter 4 closure, but its assessment reads more like a diplomatic communique than an independent analysis.

The judiciary's capacity to handle the consequences of deeper financial integration is deeply concerning. The Administrative Court has 30,000 pending cases with only 14 judges, averaging 739 days per case. The Supreme Court backlog sits at 93%, the highest in Europe. If SEPA generates additional financial disputes, consumer complaints, or financial crime cases, the court system is manifestly unable to process them in reasonable timeframes. This directly undermines the rule of law foundation that SEPA compliance is supposed to demonstrate.

On balance, the SEPA entry was the right policy decision. It delivers real cost savings, strengthens EU integration credentials, and positions Montenegro ahead of regional competitors. But the government's framing of this as an unqualified success story obscures the uncomfortable reality: Montenegro has built EU-standard financial plumbing while the enforcement and judicial infrastructure to police that plumbing remains critically underdeveloped. The risk is that Montenegro becomes an efficient corridor for both legitimate and illegitimate financial flows, and the same World Bank report that validates the cost reduction may inadvertently highlight the enforcement gap to informed observers.
critic_report.headline: SEPA Success Story Masks Montenegro's Enforcement Gap: Cheaper Payments, Same Old Money Laundering Problem
eu_chapter_relevance: Ch.4 Free Movement of Capital -- SEPA integration was a direct prerequisite for the provisional closure of this chapter in December 2025. However, sustainability depends on continued AML enforcement, which MONEYVAL found critically deficient.

Ch.9 Financial Services -- SEPA entry advances alignment but the chapter remains open. Full closure requires PSD2/PSD3 transposition, open banking frameworks, and the TIPS Clone instant payment system scheduled for mid-2026.

Ch.23 Judiciary and Fundamental Rights -- The critical rule-of-law chapter is directly implicated. MONEYVAL's finding of near-zero ML convictions and the judiciary's extreme backlogs (739-day average case resolution, 93% Supreme Court backlog) undermine the institutional foundations that SEPA compliance is supposed to demonstrate.

Ch.24 Justice, Freedom and Security -- Faster and cheaper cross-border payments expand the attack surface for financial crime, money laundering, and organized crime exploitation. The EC's 2025 report specifically called for increased efficiency in prosecution of serious and organized crime cases, especially money laundering.

Ch.17 Economic and Monetary Policy -- SEPA deepens Montenegro's de facto eurozone integration but without ECB membership or monetary policy tools. Any future EU payment regulation changes will apply to Montenegro without its input.

Ch.8 Competition Policy -- Montenegro's small banking market of 11 banks with limited competition raises questions about whether regulatory fee caps are sustainable substitutes for genuine market competition in payment services.

Ch.33 Financial and Budgetary Provisions -- The projected EUR 4.5-5 million annual reduction in corporate tax receipts from the banking sector, combined with a widening fiscal deficit (projected 3.6% of GDP in 2025), is a minor but relevant fiscal consideration.
critic_report.headline_mne: Uspješna priča o SEPA-i maskira jaz u sprovođenju propisa u Crnoj Gori: Jeftinije plaćanje, isti stari problem pranja novca
critic_report.overall_analysis_mne:
Ulazak Crne Gore u SEPA predstavlja istinsko dostignuće koje zaslužuje priznanje. Preći sa najskupljeg na najjeftiniji koridor Zapadnog Balkana za EU plaćanja za manje od godinu dana konkretan je i mjerljiv rezultat koji direktno koristi preduzećima i građanima. Nezavisna potvrda Svjetske banke daje vjerodostojnost ovom dostignuću. Poglavlje 4 (Slobodno kretanje kapitala) privremeno je zatvoreno u decembru 2025. godine dijelom zahvaljujući snazi ove reforme, a Evropski savjet za plaćanja prepoznao ga je kao ključni korak. Ovo je jedan od najjasnijih primjera u kojima je reforma orijentisana ka EU donijela opipljive i mjerljive koristi crnogorskoj ekonomiji.

Međutim, nekoliko ključnih pitanja umanjuje razloge za slavlje.

Prvo, ovo dostignuće djelimično je strukturalne prirode, a ne isključivo rezultat zalaganja. Crna Gora već jednostrano koristi euro, što joj daje inherentnu troškovnu prednost nad Albanijom (koja koristi lek) i Sjevernom Makedonijom (koja koristi denar) u SEPA transakcijama. Troškovi konverzije valuta za Crnu Goru su po defaultu nula. Poređenje SEPA troškova Crne Gore sa zemljama koje moraju i da vrše konverziju valuta je kao poređenje jabuka i krušaka. Ministarstva to univerzalno ne priznaju, tretirajući nalaz o 'najnižim troškovima' kao čisto trijumf implementacije politike.

Drugo, ključni problem koji se izbjegava jeste sprovođenje propisa. Evaluacija MONEYVAL-a iz februara 2024. bila je razorna: umjerena efikasnost u 9 od 11 oblasti, gotovo nula osuđujućih presuda za pranje novca (navodno samo dvije), a tužioci preferiraju oduzimanje imovine umjesto stvarnog krivičnog gonjenja za pranje novca. Sektor nekretnina u Crnoj Gori, koji apsorbuje više od polovine ukupnih SDI (455 miliona EUR od 890 miliona EUR u 2024. godini), MONEYVAL i Balkan Insight označili su kao primarni mehanizam za pranje prihoda od trgovine kokainom koji ostvaruju grupe poput klanova Kavač i Škaljar. Ubrzavanje i pojeftinjenje prekograničnih plaćanja bez proporcionalnog unapređenja sprovođenja AML mjera otvara širi prostor za nezakonite finansijske tokove. Ministarstvo unutrašnjih poslova i Ministarstvo pravde oba su iznijela ovu zabrinutost, što je pohvalno, ali nijedno ne pruža dokaze da se sprovođenje propisa stvarno poboljšalo od objavljivanja MONEYVAL izvještaja. Izmjene zakona o sprečavanju pranja novca usvojene su u februaru 2025, ali pravni okviri nikada nisu bili slabost Crne Gore — sprovođenje jeste.

Treće, projektovane ekonomske koristi zahtijevaju provjeru. Cifra od 38 miliona EUR godišnje uštedine (0,54% BDP-a) čini se da potiče od Centralne banke Crne Gore, a ne iz samog izvještaja Svjetske banke. Još ambicioznija projekcija od 160 miliona EUR (2,3% BDP-a, uključujući koristi TIPS Clone-a i povećanje prihoda po glavi stanovnika od 2.500 EUR tokom jedne decenije) teško je uvjerljiva bez rigoroznog metodološkog utemeljenja. Radi konteksta, BDP Crne Gore iznosi oko 7,5 milijardi EUR, a deficit opšte države projektuje se na 3,6% BDP-a u 2025. prema MMF-u. Uštedine od SEPA-e, iako realne, marginalne su u odnosu na fiskalne izazove zemlje, uključujući servisiranje duga po Euroobveznici od 850 miliona EUR emitovanoj u martu 2025. i kreditu Kineske Eximbank koji još uvijek nije otplaćen, a čini 17% javnog duga.

Četvrto, sistem instant plaćanja TIPS Clone, čije se uvođenje planira za jun-jul 2026. u saradnji sa Bankom Italije, od ključnog je značaja, ali nosi rizik od kašnjenja u implementaciji. Ako dođe do odlaganja, najambicioznije projekcije ekonomskih koristi opadaju. EBA je procijenila da je rizik od prevare kod instant kreditnih transfera do 10 puta veći nego kod konvencionalnih transfera. Bankarski sektor Crne Gore, sa svojih 11 banaka i ograničenom konkurencijom na tržištu od 620.000 stanovnika, suočava se s izazovom sprovođenja potpune AML provjere u roku od 10 sekundi koji se zahtijeva za instant plaćanja. Naslijeđeni sistemi usklađenosti nisu dizajnirani za ovakve zahtjeve.

Peto, ministarstvene procjene, mada su generalno sveobuhvatne, imaju slavodobitnički ton koji graniči s nekritičkim veličanjem. Ministarstvo finansija i Ministarstvo ekonomije oba dodjeljuju ocjene 9/10 i projektuju izrazito pozitivne ishode, ali nijedno ozbiljno ne ispituje da li su gornje granice naknada koje je propisala CBCG održive na nekonkurentnom tržištu, niti da li će banke jednostavno prebaciti troškove na druge linije proizvoda. Ocjena 9/10 Ministarstva za evropske integracije je argumentovano opravdana s obzirom na direktnu vezu sa zatvaranjem Poglavlja 4, ali njegova procjena liči više na diplomatski dopis nego na nezavisnu analizu.

Kapacitet pravosuđa za rješavanje posljedica dublje finansijske integracije ozbiljno zabrinjava. Upravni sud ima 30.000 predmeta u toku sa samo 14 sudija, a prosječno trajanje predmeta iznosi 739 dana. Zaostatak Vrhovnog suda iznosi 93%, što je najviše u Evropi. Ako SEPA generuje dodatne finansijske sporove, pritužbe potrošača ili predmete finansijskog kriminala, sudski sistem je očigledno nesposoban da ih obradi u razumnim rokovima. Ovo direktno narušava vladavinu prava na kojoj usklađenost sa SEPA-om treba da se temelji.

Sveukupno, pristupanje SEPA-i bila je ispravna politička odluka. Donosi realne uštedine troškova, jača kredencijale evropskih integracija i pozicionira Crnu Goru ispred regionalnih konkurenata. Međutim, vladino predstavljanje ovoga kao bezuslovne uspješne priče prikriva neugodnu stvarnost: Crna Gora je izgradila finansijsku infrastrukturu po EU standardu, dok infrastruktura za sprovođenje propisa i pravosuđe koji treba da kontrolišu tu infrastrukturu i dalje ostaju kritično nerazvijeni. Rizik je da Crna Gora postane efikasan koridor i za legitimne i za nezakonite finansijske tokove, a isti izvještaj Svjetske banke koji potvrđuje smanjenje troškova može nenamjerno istaći jaz u sprovođenju propisa informisanim posmatračima.
blind_spots_mne: Ovo nije vladina odluka, već retrospektivna potvrda Svjetske banke o politici implementiranoj u oktobru 2025. Ministarstva to tretiraju kao da procjenjuju novu odluku, a zapravo slave svjedočanstvo o postignutim rezultatima. Nijedno ministarstvo ne dovodi u pitanje zašto se ovo nalazi na vladinoj agendi kao stavka odluke, a ne kao informativni izvještaj.

Nijedno ministarstvo ne признаје da je troškovna prednost Crne Gore u odnosu na druge zapadnobalkanske učesnike SEPA-e (Albanija, Sjeverna Makedonija, Moldavija, koje su se istovremeno pridružile 7. oktobra 2025.) uglavnom strukturalne prirode: Crna Gora već koristi euro, čime se eliminišu troškovi konverzije valuta. Dostignuće 'najnižih troškova' dijelom je prethodno postojeća prednost koja proilazi iz euroizacije, a ne isključivo trijumf implementacije SEPA-e.

Cifra od 38 miliona EUR godišnje uštedine i projekcija od 160 miliona EUR (uključujući TIPS Clone) stalno se navode kao nalazi Svjetske banke, ali čini se da potiču od sopstvenih projekcija Centralne banke Crne Gore. Nijedno ministarstvo kritički ne ispituje metodologiju iza ovih cifara niti pravi razliku između procjena CBCG-a i nezavisno verifikovanih podataka Svjetske banke.

Nijedno ministarstvo se ne bavi rizikom koncentracije u bankarskom sektoru Crne Gore — jedanaest banaka na tržištu od 620.000 stanovnika sa ograničenom konkurencijom. Gornje granice naknada mogu privremeno suzbiti troškove, ali bez stvarne konkurencije, banke mogu kompenzovati kroz više naknade za domaće usluge van SEPA-e, naknade za održavanje računa ili kamatne marže. Održivost regulatornog određivanja cijena bez tržišne konkurencije ostaje neispitana.

Nijedno od ministarstava ne razmatra implikacije izloženosti Crne Gore u vidu 17% javnog duga prema kreditu Kineske Eximbank uz dublje finansijsko integrisanje s EU. Pitanje da li bi jeftiniji EU kapitalotokovi mogli strateški pomoći Crnoj Gori da refinansira dug od ne-EU kreditora, smanjujući geopolitičku zavisnost, propuštena je strateška tačka.

Nijedno ministarstvo ne ispituje koje su konkretne mjere za unapređenje sprovođenja AML propisa zaista implementirane od razornog izvještaja MONEYVAL-a iz februara 2024. Izmjene zakona o AML-u usvojene u februaru 2025. pominju se od strane Ministarstva pravde, ali ne postoji procjena da li se ponašanje tužilaca promijenilo, da li je Uprava za sprečavanje pranja novca dobila adekvatne resurse, ili da li se osnovna crta od dvije osuđujuće presude poboljšala u 18 mjeseci od evaluacije MONEYVAL-a.

Izvještaj Svjetske banke konkretno pokriva B2B plaćanja, ali nekoliko ministarstava (Prosvjeta, Zdravstvo, Turizam) ekstrapolira koristi na transakcije na nivou potrošača i doznake, ne praveći razliku između B2B nalaza i šireg uticaja SEPA-e na potrošače. Ovo miješanje pojmova uvećava percipirani obim validacije Svjetske banke.

Nijedno ministarstvo ne bavi se digitalnim jazom i nedostatkom finansijske pismenosti. Koristi od SEPA-e ostvaruju se kroz kanale digitalnog bankarstva, ali znatan dio crnogorskog stanovništva — posebno stariji građani i oni u sjevernim ruralnim područjima — možda ne posjeduje digitalnu pismenost ni pristup bankarskim uslugama koji su potrebni za korišćenje ovih pogodnosti, što potencijalno produbljuje ekonomsku nejednakost.
eu_chapter_relevance_mne: Pog. 4 Slobodno kretanje kapitala -- Integracija u SEPA-u bila je direktan preduslov za privremeno zatvaranje ovog poglavlja u decembru 2025. Međutim, održivost zavisi od nastavka sprovođenja AML mjera, koje je MONEYVAL ocijenio kao kritično nedovoljne.

Pog. 9 Finansijske usluge -- Pristupanje SEPA-i unapređuje usklađenost, ali poglavlje ostaje otvoreno. Za potpuno zatvaranje potrebni su transpozicija PSD2/PSD3, okviri za otvoreno bankarstvo i sistem instant plaćanja TIPS Clone čije se uvođenje planira za sredinu 2026.

Pog. 23 Pravosuđe i temeljna prava -- Ključno poglavlje o vladavini prava direktno je involvirano. Nalaz MONEYVAL-a o gotovo nula osuđujućih presuda za pranje novca i ekstremni zaostaci u pravosuđu (prosječno trajanje predmeta 739 dana, zaostatak Vrhovnog suda od 93%) potkopavaju institucionalne temelje koje usklađenost sa SEPA-om treba da demonstrira.

Pog. 24 Pravda, sloboda i bezbjednost -- Brža i jeftinija prekogranična plaćanja proširuju površinu napada za finansijski kriminal, pranje novca i eksploataciju od strane organizovanog kriminala. Izvještaj EK za 2025. konkretno je zahtijevao povećanu efikasnost u procesuiranju predmeta teškog i organizovanog kriminala, posebno pranja novca.

Pog. 17 Ekonomska i monetarna politika -- SEPA produbljuje de facto integraciju Crne Gore u eurozonu, ali bez članstva u ECB-u ili instrumenata monetarne politike. Sve buduće promjene EU regulativa o plaćanjima primjenjivaće se na Crnu Goru bez njenog učešća u odlučivanju.

Pog. 8 Politika konkurencije -- Malo bankarsko tržište Crne Gore od 11 banaka s ograničenom konkurencijom postavlja pitanje da li su regulatorne gornje granice naknada održiva zamjena za stvarnu tržišnu konkurenciju u platnim uslugama.

Pog. 33 Finansijske i budžetske odredbe -- Projektovano godišnje smanjenje prihoda od poreza na dobit bankarskog sektora od 4,5-5 miliona EUR, u kombinaciji sa rastućim fiskalnim deficitom (projiciranih 3,6% BDP-a u 2025), predstavlja manje, ali relevantno fiskalno razmatranje.

counter_proposal.title: SEPA Dividend, Security Readiness, and Competitiveness Acceleration Strategy
counter_proposal.executive_summary: Rather than treating SEPA entry as a standalone financial infrastructure achievement, the AI Government would embed it within a comprehensive strategy that (1) urgently closes the financial crime enforcement gap exposed by higher transaction volumes and speeds, (2) establishes a transparent SEPA Savings Tracking and Reinvestment Framework to channel documented cost reductions into productive investments across SME digitalization, environmental compliance, and health affordability, and (3) accelerates the TIPS Clone deployment and PSD3 regulatory alignment to drive Chapter 9 closure by late 2026, consolidating Montenegro's frontrunner EU accession status.
counter_proposal.detailed_proposal:
The World Bank report confirming Montenegro's transformation from highest to lowest cross-border payment costs in the Western Balkans is a genuine milestone. However, the government's current approach treats this as a completed achievement rather than a foundation requiring urgent complementary action. This unified counter-proposal addresses three critical gaps: security readiness, savings reinvestment, and regulatory acceleration.

PILLAR 1: FINANCIAL CRIME AND SECURITY READINESS (Priority: Urgent, Timeline: 0-12 months)

The most pressing concern raised across multiple ministries is that SEPA's faster, cheaper, higher-volume transaction environment has outpaced Montenegro's enforcement capacity. MONEYVAL's 2024 evaluation found money laundering prosecution rates near zero relative to the country's risk profile, with courts having convicted individuals in only two cases. The European Banking Authority estimates fraud risk in instant payments at up to 10 times higher than conventional transfers. This gap must be closed before the TIPS Clone system goes live in June 2026, which will further accelerate transaction speeds.

The government should establish a joint Financial Crimes Task Force comprising 20 specialized police investigators (as proposed by the Ministry of Interior) and 5-8 dedicated prosecutors (as proposed by the Ministry of Justice), co-located with the Central Bank's financial intelligence unit. This unit should be operational within 6 months, before TIPS Clone activation. All eleven commercial banks should be required to deploy real-time AI-powered transaction monitoring systems capable of AML and sanctions screening within the SEPA instant payment 10-second window, with joint Central Bank and Ministry of Interior certification. A formal information-sharing agreement with Europol's European Financial and Economic Crime Centre should be negotiated within 9 months. The target should be to triple money laundering prosecutions within two years, directly addressing MONEYVAL deficiencies and strengthening Chapter 24 (Justice, Freedom, and Security) benchmarks. Estimated cost: EUR 2-3 million annually, modest relative to the EUR 38 million in projected annual SEPA savings.

PILLAR 2: SEPA SAVINGS TRACKING AND REINVESTMENT FRAMEWORK (Timeline: 3-18 months)

Multiple ministries independently proposed earmarking SEPA savings for their sectors. Rather than creating competing claims, the government should establish a single, transparent methodology to quantify economy-wide savings from reduced transaction costs, building on the World Bank baseline data. A dedicated SEPA Dividend Fund should be created within the 2026 budget, channeling a defined portion of verified government transaction cost savings into four priority areas:

(a) SME Digitalization and Regional Inclusion (EUR 3-5 million annually): A combined initiative drawing on proposals from the Ministry of Finance and Ministry of Economy. This would fund digital payment terminal installation for tourism and trade SMEs in northern municipalities (Kolasin, Zabljak, Plav, Rozaje), provide tax credits of up to EUR 500 per business for two years for SMEs transitioning to SEPA-compliant digital invoicing, and ensure standardized SEPA-compliant accounts with transparent fee schedules are available to micro-enterprises and sole traders. The Ministry of Economy's proposal for a public dashboard tracking SEPA impact by firm size and region should be implemented to ensure benefits are not captured solely by larger, coastal businesses.

(b) Environmental Investment Acceleration (EUR 1.5-2 million annually): Drawing on the Ministry of Environment's proposal, a portion of savings should flow to the Eco Fund, which remains structurally underfunded despite the EUR 1.4 billion Chapter 27 investment requirement by 2035. This should be paired with a streamlined procurement track for EU-sourced environmental infrastructure equipment that exploits SEPA instant payments to accelerate vendor payments and attract competitive bids, particularly for the EIB water and sanitation projects in Kolasin, Rozaje, Mojkovac, Cetinje, Tivat, Kotor, and Pljevlja.

(c) Health Affordability (EUR 500,000-1 million annually): The Ministry of Health's proposal to create a Health Transaction Savings Account within the Health Insurance Fund is proportionate and practical. Documented savings from reduced pharmaceutical procurement transaction costs should fund initial copayment reforms for the 20 most commonly used chronic disease medicines, directly addressing the WHO finding that 9% of households face catastrophic health spending.

(d) Education and Brain Circulation (EUR 500,000 annually): The Ministry of Education's proposal for zero-fee SEPA transfers on verified education-related payments below EUR 5,000 is achievable through Central Bank regulatory guidance. A modest Brain Circulation Fund should finance return fellowships and compensated remote teaching engagements for Montenegrin academics abroad, leveraging the diaspora without requiring large expenditures.

The total SEPA Dividend Fund allocation of EUR 5.5-8.5 million annually represents roughly 15-22% of the projected EUR 38 million in annual economy-wide savings, a reasonable reinvestment rate that still leaves the majority of gains flowing to businesses and citizens directly.

PILLAR 3: REGULATORY ACCELERATION AND EU CHAPTER CLOSURE (Timeline: 0-18 months)

The Ministry of EU Integration correctly identifies that SEPA should be embedded within a Chapter 9 (Financial Services) closure strategy. A dedicated Chapter 9 closure task force, co-chaired by the Ministry of EU Integration and the Central Bank, should be established immediately with quarterly milestone reporting. The TIPS Clone operational launch, scheduled for June 2026 through cooperation with the Bank of Italy, should be explicitly linked to Chapter 9 benchmarks in Montenegro's next negotiating position update to Brussels.

A PSD3 regulatory gap analysis should begin immediately, ensuring Montenegro does not fall behind on the evolving EU payments acquis even as it implements PSD2. This proactive alignment would strengthen Montenegro's credibility as a frontrunner candidate, particularly given the ambitious goal of closing all remaining chapters in 2026. With 14 of 33 chapters now provisionally closed as of January 2026, and the European Commission noting that more than half the remaining work must be completed within the year, demonstrating continued momentum on financial services is critical.

The Ministry of Tourism's proposal for a "Montenegro Easy Pay" marketing brand has merit as a soft-power complement, positioning Montenegro as the most financially accessible non-EU destination in Europe. This should be coordinated with the National Tourism Organisation and Investment Promotion Agency, incorporating the FDI marketing integration proposed by the Ministry of Economy. The combination of SEPA integration, euro currency, and upcoming instant payments is a genuine competitive advantage over Serbia and Turkey that should be actively marketed to EU tour operators, digital nomads, and productive-sector investors.

BANKING SECTOR TRANSITION: The Ministry of Finance's concern about EUR 50 million in foregone bank fee income by 2028 is valid. Rather than allowing an unmanaged disruption, the government should negotiate a voluntary transition agreement with the banking association, as precedented in other SEPA accession countries. Banks should be incentivized to develop alternative revenue streams through digital payment products, while regulatory oversight ensures SEPA cost savings are fully passed through to end users rather than absorbed as new margins on other services.

REGIONAL DIMENSION: The Ministry of Economy's proposal for bilateral instant payment linkages with Serbia and Albania addresses a real gap. SEPA benefits are concentrated on EU-facing transactions while intra-Western Balkans costs remain high. Montenegro should advocate within the Berlin Process and CEFTA for extending low-cost payment corridors regionally, and should position itself as a Western Balkans payments integration knowledge hub, funded through IPA multi-country allocations as proposed by the Ministry of EU Integration.
